channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Harris and Lizzo praise Detroit — in contrast to Trump — ahead of an Atlanta rally with Usher
item.description: Vice President Kamala Harris has appeared with Lizzo in the singer's hometown of Detroit, marking the beginning of in-person voting and lavishing the city with praise after Republican nominee Donald Trump recently disparaged it.
item.dc:creator: Chris Megerian, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Minimum wage on the ballot in these 5 states
item.description: The federal minimum wage hasn't changed since 2009 but voters in several states will have the chance to raise hourly wages at the state level.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Montana Senate race, which could determine majority, seeing 'intense ground game operation': NRSC Chairman
item.description: NRSC Chairman Steve Daines told Fox News Digital in an exclusive interview he is confident Republicans will secure a Senate majority by flipping the Montana Senate seat this cycle.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The Latest: Trump and Harris are campaigning for votes in pivotal Michigan
item.description: With the Nov. 5 election fast approaching, Democrat Kamala Harris and Republican Donald Trump are setting their sights on the key battleground state of Michigan on Friday. The vice president began her day in Grand Rapids before holding events in Lansing and Oakland County, northwest of Detroit.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Montana GOP candidate who could flip control of Senate nagged by claims he lied about bullet wound
item.description: BILLINGS, Mont. (AP) — A former Park Service ranger said Friday that U.S. Senate hopeful Tim Sheehy of Montana has been lying about a bullet wound that the candidate said came from fighting in Afghanistan — going public with an accusation that has nagged the Republican’s campaign for months.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW BROWN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Home stretch: Harris, Trump hold dueling events, rallies in crucial battleground state
item.description: Vice President Kamala Harris and former President Trump held dueling campaign events in battleground Michigan with less than three weeks to go until Election Day.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump compares imprisoned Jan. 6 rioters to Japanese Americans during WWII
item.description: Nearly 900 people have pleaded guilty to a variety of federal charges in relation to the events of Jan. 6.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Texas Republicans demand answers about Venezuelan gang members
item.description: White House spokesperson Angelo Fernández Hernández has called reports Biden is "secretly flying" people into the U.S. “categorically false.
item.dc:creator: Katie Smith, NewsNation

item.title: Oregon elections office shuts down phone lines amid false info surrounding Trump, voters' pamphlet
item.description: The Oregon Elections Division is shutting down its phone lines after the system was "overwhelmed" by out-of-state callers responding to false information about why Donald Trump is not in the Oregon Voters' Pamphlet.
item.dc:creator: Anthony Kustura, Fox 5

item.title: Pro-Trump yard sign leads to shooting in Las Vegas neighborhood
item.description: Two men were playing about the former president titled "[Expletive] Donald Trump" when they decided to park their car outside the pro-Trump house.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, NewsNation

item.title: What's a 'Jezebel spirit'? Some Christians use the term to paint Kamala Harris with a demonic brush
item.description: Christian nationalist leaders are telling followers that Vice President Kamala Harris is under the influence of a “Jezebel spirit,” using a term with deeply racist and misogynistic roots that is setting off alarm bells for religious and political scholars.
item.dc:creator: ISABELLA VOLMERT and MICHELLE R. SMITH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: House Dems sue FEC accusing GOP of exploiting campaign loophole
item.description: The Democratic Congressional Campaign Committee, the House Democrats campaign arm, filed its lawsuit against the FEC on Thursday, alleging Republicans are using a campaign finance loophole in their television ads.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: British invasion: Labour Party to descend on US to help Harris
item.description: In a now-deleted social media post, the head of operations for the British Labour Party indicated "nearly 100" current and former party staffers will be headed to the United States to help Vice President Kamala Harris during the final stretch before the election.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Former Minnesota Congressman Rick Nolan dies at age 80
item.description: Former U.S. Rep. Rick Nolan, who represented two of Minnesota’s congressional districts in Washington more than 30 years apart, has died at the age of 80.
item.dc:creator: MPR News Staff and Dan Kraker, MPR News

item.title: Barbed wire, high fences: some election spots in Arizona close, but others step up
item.description: Some election locations in Arizona are shutting their doors in the face of security threats — but others are expanding their footprint.
item.dc:creator: Ximena Bustillo, MPR News

item.title: Harris: Trump 'has no idea' about women's health care
item.description: “The man calls himself the father of IVF? I mean what does that even mean?” said Harris, referencing her rival’s comments on in vitro fertilization (IVF).
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: At Wisconsin stops, Harris campaign features less talk of joy and more attacks on Trump
item.description: In La Crosse on Thursday, Harris told a rally crowd that Trump “will stop at nothing to claim unchecked power for himself.” It’s a shift from the “joy” that swirled around her elevation to the top of the Democratic ticket this summer.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: SBA administrator visits Tampa Bay as disaster loan program runs dry
item.description: While visiting businesses in Tampa Bay, which included both of those businesses, SBA Administrator Isabel Casillas Guzman said the next move is up to members of Congress.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Candidate Karl Catarata talks education, safety, and accountability in Clark County School Board race
item.description: Karl Catarata, running for the District A trustee seat on the Clark County School Board, joined the 8 News Now Daily Voting Guide for an interview to discuss his platform and vision for the district.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Almost 23% of people say politics influences where they live: Poll
item.description: "Some go so far as to consider moving to find that alignment," Danielle Hale, chief economist for Realtor.com, said.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, Fox 5

item.title: 3 states renew efforts to limit abortion pill access
item.description: The lawsuit was filed in a Texas federal court Friday, roughly four months after the Supreme Court decided against undoing changes to the FDA’s regulations on the abortion pill that expanded access — such as allowing it to be mailed without an in-person doctor’s visit.
item.dc:creator: Alejandra O'Connell-Domenech, Fox 5

item.title: Illinois alderwoman claims she was arrested after criticizing the mayor
item.description: Alderwoman Colby Chapman said she spent five hours in jail, in what she calls a politically motivated abuse of power.
item.dc:creator: Gabriel Castillo, NewsNation

item.title: WATCH: Nevada US Senate Debate: Jacky Rosen and Sam Brown
item.description: As the seconds tick away until Election Day on Nov. 5, Democratic incumbent Sen. Jacky Rosen and Republican challenger Sam Brown are set to debate the issues Nevadans are most concerned about heading into 2025 and beyond.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Oklahoma politicians debate ethics of 'paddling' students
item.description: There were 351 reported instances of Oklahoma students with disabilities who were stricken in schools during the 2020-21 school year.
item.dc:creator: Markie Martin, NewsNation

item.title: How to watch the Nevada US Senate Debate
item.description: The only debate between Democratic incumbent Sen. Jacky Rosen and Republican challenger Sam Brown will occur on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: JD Vance says 'no,' he does not think Trump lost in 2020
item.description: Vance has faced repeated questions on the campaign trail about whether Trump lost in 2020. On Wednesday, he gave his most direct answer to date, answering "no" in response to a reporter's question.
item.dc:creator: Jason Breslow, MPR News

item.title: Mayor Johnson's chief of staff says school CEO and board kept 5th Floor in the dark
item.description: In an exclusive sitdown, Cristina Pacione-Zayas invited WGN to City Hall’s 5th floor to, in her words, “clear the air.
item.dc:creator: Tahman Bradley, WGN 9

item.title: Trump would be the oldest person to become president. He’s not sharing health details
item.description: If he wins next month’s election, Donald Trump would be the oldest person in U.S. history elected president. Yet the 78-year-old Republican nominee is refusing to disclose new details about his physical or mental well-being, breaking decades of precedent.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Nevada political parties seek to sway ‘Gen Z’ amid inflation and recovery from an on-campus mass shooting
item.description: Young Democrats and College Republicans at UNLV are campaigning for their respective candidates in the 2024 election, citing issues such as inflation, gun violence, and reproductive freedoms, while the Trump campaign is targeting students through social media and face-to-face meetings.
item.dc:creator: James Schaeffer, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Nebraska court allows voter registrations from people with felony convictions
item.description: Nebraskans with felony convictions may register to vote after the state's highest court ruled Wednesday that a top election official had no authority to deem a law restoring those rights unconstitutional.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Canceling subscriptions has to be as easy as signing up, FTC says in a new rule
item.description: The "click-to-cancel" rule, now finalized by the Federal Trade Commission, aims to make it easier to cancel subscriptions and memberships. Companies argue the agency overstepped its authority.
item.dc:creator: Alina Selyukh, MPR News

item.title: Donald Trump addresses Economic Club of Chicago
item.description: CHICAGO — Former President Donald Trump is back in Chicago Tuesday. Trump addressed the Economic Club of Chicago at Fairmont Chicago, located at 200 North Columbus Drive, ahead of the Nov. 5 election.
item.dc:creator: Christine Flores, WGN 9

item.title: Johnson: Additional hurricane aid 'can wait' until Congress is back in session
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) on Sunday said passing additional hurricane aid for states impacted by Hurricanes Helene and Milton “can wait” until Congress is back in session after the election.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump hears at a Latino campaign event from someone who lived in the US illegally
item.description: Minutes after Donald Trump delivered his standard warnings about drug dealers and criminals illegally crossing the border during a Saturday campaign event, the former president heard from someone who was once in the country illegally but now plans to vote for him.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS BEAUMONT and NICHOLAS RICCARDI, Associated Press, Fox 5
